name: base_drawer_cabinet
notes: Whenever possible, the bottom/base panel passes under the side panels at full outside width.
Interior dividers stay inside the carcass between base/top members.
Use half-edge overlay fronts with up to 9 mm overlap per adjacent carcass edge.
Leave at least 3 mm reveal where two fronts meet over the same carcass edge.
Band only visible edges by default: front edge on carcass members and all four edges on doors/drawer fronts.
Add side edge banding only when a side is visible, such as islands or exposed end panels.
Mark and count 35 mm hinge cup drilling on doors.
Cabinet backs are 3 mm and pass over the carcass at full outside module width and height.
Maximize drawer box width with 13 mm slide clearance on each side.
Lay out each drawer box from its own front: the drawer bottom starts at least 10 mm above that front's lower edge.
Use equal drawer box heights within a stack unless different depths/heights are requested.
Leave at least 4 mm clearance below the lowest drawer box and above the highest drawer box.
Extra leftover vertical clearance may go above the top drawer.
Drawer bottoms are 5 mm, pass under the drawer box parts, and use the full outside drawer-box footprint.
Drawer sides, trasfrente, and contrafrente sit on top of the pass-through drawer bottom.
Drawer sides, trasfrente, and contrafrente have top-edge banding because that edge is visible when open.
Place top supports inside the carcass.
Keep visible front heights aligned to neighboring gola lines.
Export every front, rail, side, drawer box member, and back panel as individual objects.
